description: Imagine being able to support a healthier aging brain with just a simple vitamin - that's what a recent study out of Japan is suggesting, and it's got a lot of people in the medical community talking. Researchers looked at over 2,000 older adults and found that people with lower vitamin C levels in their blood tended to have less gray matter and weaker connections in a key brain network involved in memory, attention, and other cognitive functions. title: Health News Jun 29 — Osteopenia is silently weakening bones in mil
description: You're just one brittle bone away from a potentially life-altering fracture, and millions of people are living with this reality due to a condition known as osteopenia. It's a silent threat that's often overlooked, causing bones to become less dense and more fragile over time. The scary part is that many people only discover they have osteopenia after a fracture or a bone scan, which is why it's crucial to understand the risk factors. Aging, menopause, a poor diet, and inactivity can all contrib...

title: Health News Jun 29 — Osteopenia is silently weakening bones in mil
description: Breaking news in the world of health: millions of people are silently developing a condition that's weakening their bones, and it's not just about osteoporosis. Osteopenia is a common but often overlooked condition that causes bones to become less dense and more fragile, and because it develops silently, many people only discover they have it after a fracture or bone scan.
